observations.recommendation:
1.11 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Demand the immediate liquidation of cash advances, which
were already due for liquidation;
b. Cause the deduction from the salaries or any claims of the
concerned AOs who failed to liquidate their outstanding cash
advances within 30 days from receipt of the demand letter;
c. Strengthen controls on the monitoring of the grant and
utilization of cash advances of the Municipality; and
d. Record in the books of accounts the liquidation of
confidential funds as follows:
Particulars Amount
Prior Period Adjustment ₱ 6,453,268.00
Advances to Special Disbursing Officers ₱ 6,453,268.00
To record the liquidated confidential funds.

2.8 We recommended and the:
2.8.1 Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Effect the necessary adjusting entry in the books of
accounts, as follows:
CIP - Infrastructure Assets ₱1,596,588.87
Buildings ₱1,596,588.87
b. Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and MPDC prior to
recording of projects as completed; and
2.8.2 Municipal Engineer and MPDC agreed to establish a
standardized reporting system that clearly indicates the
status of projects to prevent discrepancies in financial
reporting and ensure compliance with audit and accounting
standards.

3.9 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Reclassify the improperly recorded non-trade payables from
Accounts Payable to their appropriate liability accounts, with
the following suggested accounting entry:
Particulars Amount
Accounts Payable ₱ 1,442,585.44
Due to Officers and Employees ₱ 362,935.44
Other Payables 1,079,650.00
To reclassify non-trade payables from the Accounts Payable
account to the appropriate liability accounts.
b. Henceforth, observe the proper classification of accounts in
recording financial transactions as prescribed in the RCA for
LGUs under COA Circular No. 2015-009.

4.13 We recommended and the:
4.13.1 Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Prepare the adjusting entries to properly recognize the
prior year’s unrecorded income;
b. Review thoroughly the RCDs, before closing the books of
accounts, to determine if collections of the year are
accurately and completely reported;
4.13.2 Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
a. Require all revenue collectors of the Municipality,
including the Barangay Treasurers, to remit their
collections within the prescribed period;
b. Monitor the preparation of the Report of Collections and
Deposit to ensure its timely and complete submission to
the Municipal Accounting Office; and
c. Deposit all collections to the depository bank in a timely
and complete manner.

5.9 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. In coordination with the appropriate Municipal Offices:
   i. Give technical support and guidance to the barangays in
   the preparation of liquidation documents;
   ii. Assess the eligibility of recipient barangays in terms of
   their capability to implement the respective programs,
   projects, and activities to prevent delays in completion
   and ensure timely liquidation of fund transfers;
b. Issue regularly demand letters and conduct follow-ups to
barangays with outstanding balances, in coordination with
the Municipal Local Government Operations Officer to compel
compliance; and
c. Process fund transfers for barangays with no unsettled
amounts, and issue certifications to confirm compliance.

6.9 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Trace the details of the amounts not sourced from other LGUs
to properly account for and support these balances; and
b. Reclassify the donations and receipts from solicitations to the
appropriate income account or as prior period adjustments to
government equity.

7.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with
the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Engineer, agreed to
exert efforts in tracing the details of the amount and effect the necessary
adjusting entries in the books of accounts.

8.9 We recommended and the:
8.9.1 Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and determine
the actual status of the outstanding checks;
b. Take up the adjustments in the books of accounts for
checks that have become stale for a longer period, and no
manifestation for replacement from the concerned payees
was received;
c. Henceforth, inform the Municipal Treasurer of any
outstanding checks identified in the Bank Reconciliation
Statements so that appropriate action could be
undertaken in their end;
8.9.2 Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
a. Send written notices to the concerned payees; and
b. Issue a new check upon submission by the payees of the
spoiled or stale checks.

9.8 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer agreed to:
a. Conduct regular reconciliation between their respective
records;
b. Investigate the purpose of the account in the PNB; and
c. As members of the Local Finance Committee, present this
dormant account to the Local Sanggunian for its proper
disposition.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit immediately the reconciliation and disclosures on the disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2023 regarding Reconciliation between Actual Amounts on a Comparable Basis as presented in Statement of Comparison of Budgets and Actual Amounts (SCABAA) and in the Statement of Financial Performance; total road network systems; and property, plant and equipment and ensure to timely submit these reports in the succeeding years.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer/Project Team agreed to solve the issues on right-of-way (ROW) and project design; and also, the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator agreed to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate sufficient funds on projects that are not yet implemented during calendar year 2023. Likewise, the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure that development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement and implementation ready.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) avail of the remedies by administrative action through levy on real property or by judicial action to collect delinquent taxes as provide in RA 7160; and (b) strengthen and intensify its tax campaign to minimize tax delinquencies.

observations.managementAction: Management acknowledges the status of the observation, wherein the Contractor's completion deadline was extended from the original date of February 11, 2021, due to additional works necessitated post-inspection by the Inspectorate team. This extension was prompted by modifications in the program of works, such as the exclusion of cabinet doors and drawers in certain rooms, as outlined in a variation order. Instead, new project items were introduced, including the installation of extended roofing and framing to mitigate rain ingress into the Rural Health Unit facade, the incorporation of louvers and fire exits, and the provision of cabinets and drawers urgently required by some RHU offices. Variation NO. 01, dated February 2021, received by COA on March 16, 2021, officially documents these alterations. Despite these changes, management opted not to impose liquidated damages, deeming the circumstances acceptable and advantageous to the government.

observations.recommendation: 1.8	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that cash advances of the concerned accountable officers are liquidated in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and strengthen internal control in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. In addition, exhaust all possible remedies and demand immediate liquidation of the forgoing unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal GSO maintain property cards and direct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to reconcile any discrepancy between the balances in the books and the result of the physical count of the PPE accounts. Make necessary adjustments in their records to take up any noted variances.
We recommended further, that the GSO together with the Inventory Committee created for the purpose regularly conduct physical count of PPE and submit reports thereto in accordance with the provisions stated above.

3.7	In view of the foregoing, we recommended that:
3.7.1	The Municipal Accountant determine those projects which are still lodged in the CIP account but were actually completed and recognize the appropriate asset account and the related depreciation thereon; and
3.7.2	The Municipal Engineering Office submit and coordinate timely with MAO on the status of the on-going and completed projects and its supporting documents to properly classify and present a more accurate account balance of the CIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Finance Committee prepare the report monthly and ensure to post the same to LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month as set by the provisions stated above.

5.8	We recommended that the members of the BAC equip and update themselves of the existing GPPB guidelines which are exempted from the provisions of RA No. 9184 in response to COVID-19 outbreak and refrain from acquiring goods by means of Shopping as mode of procurement with projects above the threshold set in RA 9184 unless otherwise exempt during emergency cases.

6.8 	We recommended that the Management require the supplier to submit a valid Mayor’s or Business Permit, OSS and Income Tax Returns or Business Tax Returns as required by the foregoing provision of GPPB Circular        No. 01-2020.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee headed by the BAC Chairman strictly adhere to the regulations set in COA Circular 2009-001 in submission of copy of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the office of the auditor for the latter to conduct a timely review of perfected contracts.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and Bids and Awards Committee strengthen its control in submission of a sufficient, clearly and properly accomplished forms to the transactions specified in Section 4(6) of PD 1445 in order to avoid suspension in audit.  Moreover, strictly follow the guidelines provided in the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs on how to complete the necessary forms for the different transactions to ascertain the validity and propriety of the claim.

9.8	We recommended and the Management agreed to adhere the provisions set forth under COA Circular 2020-009 by submitting the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances within the prescribed period with supporting documents.

10.12	We recommended that the Management strictly implement actions to strengthen its collection and monitoring procedures and direct Local Finance Committee to undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual budget on the basis of reasonable and realistic projection of revenues and conduct an analysis of identified revenue accounts with decreasing trend in collection.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and other concerned personnel exert all efforts to register all land owned by the Municipality under the Torrens Title System in conformity with COA Circular No. 92-386.
Further, reconciliation between the records of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor should be regularly made to accurately establish the valuation of land in the books of accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office conduct reconciliation of the balances of Cash-Local Treasury and their respective books at least once every quarter.

The Municipal Treasure’s Office retrieve and immediately cancel the original copy of the official receipt together with the RCD pertaining to the collection of staled check and submit to the accounting office for adjustments.

The Municipal Treasurer immediately deposit the undeposited collections noted and ensure that future deposits are prompt and intact.
